netbird_disabled: NetBird is disabled. Enable it in the Settings tab.
connection.title: Connection
connection.connect_info: Connect or disconnect from the NetBird network:
settings.setup_key_placeholder: Leave empty to authenticate via browser
settings.context.unraid_listen: When enabled, Unraid services (WebGUI, SSH, SMB) will listen on the NetBird interface IP in addition to other interfaces.
settings.context.ip_forward: Enables kernel IP forwarding, required if you want to use NetBird as a router or route traffic through this host.
settings.context.management_url: The NetBird management server URL. Use the default for the NetBird cloud service, or enter your self-hosted management server URL.
settings.context.setup_key: A NetBird setup key for automatic registration without browser authentication. Leave empty to authenticate via browser.
settings.context.admin_url: URL of the NetBird admin dashboard for easy access.
settings.context.hosts: Adds NetBird peer hostnames and IP addresses to /etc/hosts for DNS resolution without MagicDNS.
settings.context.save: Saving settings will restart the NetBird daemon.
settings.context.restart: Restart the NetBird daemon to apply changes.
settings.context.diagnostics: Download a diagnostics bundle to help troubleshoot issues.
settings.context.erase: Stop NetBird, remove the configuration and state files, and reset to factory defaults. This will require re-authentication.
settings.context.reauthenticate: Trigger re-authentication with the management server.
status.name: Name
warnings.not_ready: NetBird daemon is not ready. This page will retry automatically about once per minute; you can also refresh manually.
warnings.connected_via_netbird: Connect/disconnect actions are disabled because you are currently connected via NetBird. Performing these actions would disconnect you.
